opportunity_summary: Google Hamina is a landmark seawater-cooled hyperscale DC built in a former paper mill since 2009. Uses Gulf of Finland seawater pumped through pipes up to 2m diameter, via renovated 20-year-old pumps. Water-to-water heat exchange modules. EUR 3.5B invested to date. 7th DC under construction. Heat recovery with Haminan Energia supplies ~80% of city heating via 7.5MW heat pump. Pioneering seawater cooling design.
confidence_reason: Google official data center page confirms Hamina location, seawater cooling, EUR 3.5B investment, 7th DC construction, Haminan Energia heat recovery. DCK, DCD, and Wilo case study corroborate.

source_2_evidence_snippet: Seawater from Gulf of Finland pumped through pipes up to 2m diameter. Water-to-water heat exchange modules. Gravity-fed return. Renovated paper mill pumps.
notes: Built in former Summa paper mill. Pioneering seawater cooling design. Return water mixed with incoming seawater before discharge to minimize thermal impact. Haminan Energia heat pump extracts ~80% of Hamina city's district heating needs from DC waste heat. EUR 3.5B cumulative investment makes this one of Finland's largest single-site investments.

opportunity_summary: Colt DCS London 4 is a 31MW hyperscale facility at Hayes Digital Park. Designed for cloud and AI/HPC workloads. PUE <1.3 target. 9 data halls across 5 floors. BREEAM Very Good target. 100% renewable energy PPA. Waste heat recovery and water recycling planned. Phase 1 of larger Hayes campus expanding to 160MW (London 4, 6, 7, 8). GBP 2.5B total investment. London 6/7/8 add 97MW, construction mid-2026, L6 live early 2029.
confidence_reason: Colt DCS official site and PR Newswire confirm 31MW, PUE <1.3, 9 halls, 5 floors, BREEAM target. Techerati and DCD confirm GBP 2.5B investment and 160MW campus total.

source_2_evidence_snippet: GBP 2.5B investment. London 6/7/8 add 97MW taking Hayes to 160MW total. Construction mid-2026. London 6 live early 2029.

notes: One of the largest DC investments in UK AI economy. London 4 is first phase; London 6/7/8 approved for additional 97MW. Green walls and screening panels. Smart building controls for cooling efficiency optimization. Cundall is engineering consultant.

opportunity_summary: Telehouse North Two in London Docklands. World's first multi-storey adiabatic cooling system. PUE 1.16 in N+2 configuration. 18.5MW capacity with 132kV grid and 8 N+1 generators. BREEAM Excellent. 530+ carriers, ISPs, ASPs. Europe's leading connectivity hub. Opened 2016.
confidence_reason: Telehouse official site confirms PUE 1.16, multi-storey adiabatic cooling, 18.5MW, 132kV, N+2, BREEAM Excellent. Baxtel and Wikipedia corroborate.
source_1_title: Telehouse North Two Data Centre
source_1_evidence_snippet: World's first multi-storey adiabatic cooling. PUE 1.16, N+2. 18.5MW. 132kV grid. BREEAM Excellent. 530+ carriers.
source_2_title: Telehouse London Docklands Campus Brochure
source_2_evidence_snippet: 18.5MW standby capacity from 8 N+1 generators. 132kV grid substation on campus.
notes: Part of the larger Telehouse London Docklands campus (North, East, West buildings). World's first multi-storey indirect adiabatic cooling system is a notable engineering achievement. KDDI-owned. Telehouse West Two (33MW, GBP 275M) broke ground Oct 2025 for 2028 completion.

opportunity_summary: Telehouse West Two is a GBP 275M, 33MW new build on the London Docklands campus. 9-storey, 32,000 sqm total, 11,292 sqm white space across 6 levels. Integrates both air and liquid cooling for high-density compute. Two 132kV substations. 4.4MW per floor. BREEAM Excellent target. 100% renewable energy. Floor-by-floor ventilation and chilled water cooling. Completion 2028.
confidence_reason: Telehouse official announcement and multiple industry outlets (DCD, Data Centre Magazine, Capacity Global, PM Today) confirm 33MW, GBP 275M, liquid+air cooling, 132kV, BREEAM Excellent, 2028 completion.
source_1_title: Telehouse breaks ground on new GBP 275M Telehouse West Two
source_1_evidence_snippet: 33MW, 9-storey, 32,000 sqm. Air + liquid cooling. Two 132kV substations. 4.4MW/floor. BREEAM Excellent. 100% renewable. Completion 2028.

source_2_evidence_snippet: Telehouse West Two, GBP 275M, 33MW, groundbreaking October 2025, completion 2028.
notes: Major new investment for Telehouse/KDDI in London Docklands. Liquid cooling integration designed from the start for high-density compute environments. Joins existing North, East, West buildings on campus. 11,292 sqm white space is substantial.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Telehouse breaks ground on GBP 275M Telehouse West Two. 33MW. Completion 2028.

opportunity_summary: DataVita DV1 in North Lanarkshire is Scotland's largest data center. Current 40MW capacity with 500MW+ expansion planned. Supports direct-to-chip, air, and immersive cooling. Adiabatic cooling (Scotland avg 8C year-round eliminates mechanical cooling need). Design PUE 1.18 full load; new expansion targets 1.15. Closed-loop cooling, near-zero water consumption. UK AI Growth Zone designation. 35% lower operating costs than other European hubs.
confidence_reason: DataVita official site confirms 500MW+ expansion, DTC/air/immersion cooling, PUE 1.15-1.18, closed-loop near-zero water. FutureScot confirms GBP 8M investment. Scottish Construction Now confirms AI Growth Zone. Datacentermap confirms 40MW.

source_1_evidence_snippet: Over 500MW new capacity across Scotland. Liquid and air cooling for super high density. PUE 1.15. Closed-loop, near-zero water. 35% lower than EU hubs.
source_2_title: DV1 Data Centre Lanarkshire
source_2_evidence_snippet: DV1 in North Lanarkshire. Tier III colocation. Scotland's climate avg 8C enables indirect adiabatic cooling year-round. Design PUE 1.18 full load.
notes: DataVita also operates DV2 in Glasgow city center (177 Bothwell Street). Scotland's cool climate (avg 8C) is a key competitive advantage. North Lanarkshire site named UK AI Growth Zone. Modular designs for rapid deployment (4-6 weeks). 35% cost advantage vs other EU hubs due to renewable energy and climate.
expansion_details: 500MW+ new capacity across Scotland. Modular deployment in 4-6 weeks. UK AI Growth Zone.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Over 500 MW of new data centre capacity across Scotland, purpose-built for demanding workloads.

Low energy indirect evaporative cooling with borehole water extraction. Major financial and enterprise connectivity hub. Part of extensive London LD series (LD1-LD13+).

opportunity_summary: EcoDataCenter Borlange (Kvarnsveden) is a mega campus under construction on 20 hectares of former papermill land. 250MW initial capacity scaling to 600MW. EUR 600M debt financing secured. Ground broken September 2025, first DC completion early 2027. Up to 9 buildings planned. 100% renewable energy. District heating integration can offset up to 25% of cooling spend. EcoDataCenter offers air, DLC, and immersion cooling across portfolio.

opportunity_summary: Bahnhof Sterik (St Erik) is a 21MW facility in Stockholm with 120+ racks, 50 of which support true high-density loads in power and cooling. Recently expanded and mechanically overhauled with new genset and switchboard. High-density capability makes it a potential liquid cooling retrofit candidate. 25kW/rack capacity on 42U racks.
confidence_reason: Datacentermap and Baxtel confirm 21MW, 120+ racks, 50 high-density racks, recent mechanical overhaul. No PUE published. Limited primary source data.

source_2_evidence_snippet: 21MW power capacity. 42U racks at 25kW/rack.
notes: Part of Bahnhof's Stockholm portfolio alongside Pionen. High-density capability (50 racks) suggests potential for liquid cooling adoption if AI workloads increase. Recent mechanical overhaul is a good sign for vendor engagement. Bahnhof scrapped its Elementica (21MW) Stockholm project to focus on Gothenburg instead.

full_address: Lyseparken, Bjornafjorden municipality, near Bergen, Norway
opportunity_summary: Lyseparken / The Spark is a Snohetta-designed energy-positive data center concept near Bergen. 2MW, 200-rack pilot facility. Liquid cooling system sends excess heat to district heating for surrounding 600,000 sqm business park. Closed-loop: heated liquid heats buildings, cools in transit, returns to DC. Designed to power cities up to 18,000 people. 40% overall energy reduction. Solar-powered buildings feed energy back. 3,000-5,000 new houses planned alongside.
confidence_reason: Fast Company, Inhabitat, and Architect Magazine confirm Snohetta design, 2MW spec, liquid cooling, energy-positive concept. Lyseparken.webflow.io confirms development. Project status unclear â€” original target was 2021, may be delayed.
source_1_title: This town will get its heat from an unlikely source: a data center
source_1_evidence_snippet: Lyseparken near Bergen. 2MW, 200 racks. Liquid cooling sends heat to district heating. Closed-loop return. 600,000 sqm business park. 3,000-5,000 houses.
source_2_title: Snohetta designs an energy-positive data center
source_2_evidence_snippet: The Spark prototype designed to power cities of up to 18,000 people. 40% energy reduction.
notes: Innovative concept: world's first energy-positive city with DC at center. Snohetta is internationally renowned architecture firm. Bjornafjorden municipality developing Lyseparken with infrastructure for large-scale DC heat recycling from day one. Original target date was 2021 â€” current status needs verification. Small 2MW facility but high innovation value.
heat_recovery_details: Core concept: liquid cooling closed-loop feeds district heating for entire business park. Buildings closest to DC get most heat. Liquid cools in transit, returns to DC. 40% energy reduction.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Lyseparken development with data center heat recycling infrastructure from the ground up.
